title: VerificationReport
description: The machine-readable audit artifact produced by pipeline Step 5 (Verification Layer). Written to verification/report.json by both verification/run.js and integration/trunk-pipeline.js. This report is the definitive record of whether a trunk's output is grounded, what proof was available, what checks were run, and what (if anything) must block continuation. The 'pass' boolean is the gate: any downstream system that acts on trunk output MUST check this field first. The companion evidence_tree.md is the human-readable version of this same data.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for the pipeline run that produced this report. Generated by runPipeline() in verification/pipeline.js. Format: 'run-<epoch-ms>-<random-7-chars>'. Matches the run_id in the ContextPacket and GroundingPlan for this turn. Used to join this report with other per-run artifacts (evidence_tree.md, packet logs).

timestamp_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the verification run was executed. Must be UTC. This is the authoritative timestamp for audit log ordering — not when the trunk generated output, but when the verifier checked it.

pass.description: Overall pass/fail gate. TRUE only when every item in results[] has passed=true. FALSE when any check failed. Any downstream system — the orchestrator, the next trunk, the clinician portal — MUST treat pass=false as a hard block. It is a schema error to act on trunk output when pass=false. Note: pass=true does not mean the output is clinically correct — it means the output is grounded and the verifier found no fabricated claims. Clinical correctness is evaluated separately by the scoring layer against the synthetic patient record nodes.

results.description: Per-check verification results. Exactly one item per check run. The current verifier.js runs exactly 5 checks in fixed order. This array should therefore contain exactly 5 items for a standard pipeline run. The check name enum is fixed to the 5 checks implemented in verifier.js — extending the verifier with new checks must add new enum values here.

check.description: The name of the verification check. Fixed enum mapping to the 5 checks in verifier.js verify(). no_invented_codes: detects SNOMED/ICD codes in output without a terminology lookup receipt. no_invented_guidelines: detects 'Choosing Wisely/eTG says...' claims without a docs.cite ID. no_invented_operations: detects IHI/lab/pharmacy/delivery claims without a live-data receipt. no_repo_invention: detects backtick-quoted internal service names not in the allowed list. hard_stop_enforcement: detects HARD_FAIL or 'critical acuity override' in output without a pharmacology or investigation receipt to back it.

passed.description: Whether this specific check passed. A single false here causes pass=false at the report level.

reason.description: Optional. Present when passed=false. Human-readable explanation of why this check failed. Drawn from the reason field in verifier.js results. Should be specific enough for a developer to identify the exact offending claim in the trunk output.
examples: output contains code-like references; terminology receipt required
output contains guideline claims; docs.cite required
output contains operational claims; live receipt required

severity.description: Optional. Severity classification for this check when it fails. Assigned per check name: no_invented_codes=critical (wrong clinical codes are a patient safety and medicolegal failure), no_invented_guidelines=fail (wrong guideline = clinical risk but not necessarily immediate harm), no_invented_operations=critical (fabricated IHI or lab results are an identity and safety failure), no_repo_invention=warning (architecture leak but not directly patient-facing), hard_stop_enforcement=critical (ignoring a HARD_FAIL is a direct patient safety failure). Only meaningful when passed=false — omit or ignore when passed=true.

evidence_refs_checked.description: Optional. The receipt request_ids or citation_ids that the verifier checked against for this check. Useful for tracing exactly which receipts the verifier had available when it ran this check. Populated from the evidence/citations/receipts passed to verify() in verifier.js.

missing_receipts.description: List of receipts or citations that were required by the trunk output but not found in the ContextPacket. Each string identifies one missing artifact. Drawn from missing_receipts in verifier.js verify() return. This is the primary actionable output for the retrieval layer — if a re-run is attempted, these are exactly what needs to be fetched. Empty array when pass=true.
examples: terminology.lookup receipt required for any SNOMED/ICD code
docs.cite ID required for guideline claims
live-data receipt required for IHI/lab/pharmacy/delivery claims
HARD_FAIL or critical acuity override requires pharmacology/investigation receipt
output must not introduce repo names outside gap register: deep-library-agent

trunk_id.description: Optional. The trunk whose output was verified. Present in reports produced by integration/trunk-pipeline.js (runTrunkWithGrounding). Absent in reports produced by the standalone verification/run.js script. When present, enables per-trunk pass-rate tracking across a case set.

session_ref.description: Optional. The encounter/session ID. Present in reports produced by runTrunkWithGrounding when sessionRef is supplied. Matches ContextGraph.encounter_id and ContextPacket.session_ref. Enables joining all verification reports for a single consultation.

overall_severity.description: Optional. A single coarse severity label for the whole report, derived from the worst-severity failed check. Derivation rule: if any check with severity='critical' failed → 'critical'; else if any check with severity='fail' failed → 'fail'; else if any check with severity='warning' failed → 'warning'; else → 'pass'. 'critical' severity means the output poses a direct patient safety or identity risk and must never be forwarded. 'fail' means the output is ungrounded but not immediately dangerous. 'warning' means a policy violation was found but the clinical content may be valid. 'pass' means all checks passed.

hard_stops.description: Optional. Affirmative danger signals found in the trunk output — distinct from missing_receipts (which are absences) these are active HARD_FAIL or 'critical acuity override' assertions that must block continuation regardless of other check results. Populated when hard_stop_enforcement check detects these patterns. When non-empty and overall_severity='critical', the next trunk or orchestrator must halt and escalate.
examples: HARD_FAIL: pharm.check returned HARD_FAIL for aspirin + documented NSAID allergy
critical acuity override: output recommends immediate ED transfer — escalation required

candidate_output_excerpt.description: Optional. A short excerpt (first 500 chars) of the trunk output that was verified. Not the full output — this is for audit trail identification only, not for replay. Enables a human reviewer to identify which specific output version this report covers without re-running the pipeline.

candidate_output_hash.description: Optional. SHA-256 hash of the full candidate output text, prefixed with 'sha256:'. This is the immutable medicolegal audit anchor — if the trunk output is later disputed, this hash proves exactly what was verified. Format: 'sha256:<64-hex-chars>'. Should be computed by the verifier before any processing of the output string.

packet_summary.description: Optional. Key metrics from the ContextPacket used in this verification run. Enables audit trail reconstruction without storing the full packet in the report.

facts_count.description: Number of facts[] items in the ContextPacket.

evidence_count.description: Number of EvidenceNode items in the ContextPacket.

receipts_count.description: Number of Receipt items in the ContextPacket.receipts[].

static_doc_citations.description: Citation IDs from static_doc EvidenceNode supports that were available to the verifier.

live_receipt_ids.description: request_ids of live_data_receipt EvidenceNode supports available to the verifier.

packet_mode.description: Effective mode of the ContextPacket — from ContextPacket.mode. Reports produced in mock mode should be clearly distinguishable from live-mode reports in audit logs.

packet_blocked.description: Whether the ContextPacket had blocked=true at assembly time. If true and generation proceeded anyway, that is itself a verification failure.

pipeline_version.description: Optional. The version of the grounding pipeline schema set that produced this report. Matches the schema_version in case_envelope.json for synthetic records. Allows migration handling if the schema set is updated.

mode.description: Optional. Effective mode inherited from the ContextPacket. 'mock' reports are generated during development and testing. Only 'live' reports should be used for production audit or medicolegal purposes.

current_code_shape.run_js: Writes { run_id, timestamp_utc, pass, results, missing_receipts } to verification/report.json. All 5 required fields present.
current_code_shape.trunk_pipeline_js: Writes { run_id, timestamp_utc, trunk_id, session_ref, pass, results, missing_receipts } as the 'report' key of the runTrunkWithGrounding() return. Superset of run.js shape.
current_code_shape.backwards_compatibility: Both existing output shapes are valid against this schema — required fields are exactly the 5 present in run.js. trunk_id and session_ref are optional, matching their presence in trunk-pipeline but absence in run.js.

produced_by: verification/run.js — standalone script, writes 5-field minimal report
integration/trunk-pipeline.js — runTrunkWithGrounding(), writes 7-field report including trunk_id and session_ref
consumed_by: Any downstream system deciding whether to act on trunk output — must check pass=true first
Clinician verification portal — reviews hard_stops and missing_receipts
Coverage analysis tooling — aggregates per-trunk pass rates across synthetic case sets
Medicolegal audit — candidate_output_hash provides immutable proof of what was verified
_integration_notes.companion_artifact: verification/evidence_tree.md — human-readable Markdown version of this same data, also written by run.js and trunk-pipeline.js

schema_refs.produced_from_packet: context-packet.schema.json (File 4) — packet.evidence and packet.receipts are the proof inputs to verify()
schema_refs.produced_from_plan: grounding-plan.schema.json (File 3) — plan.run_id propagates to this report
schema_refs.check_logic_source: verification/verifier.js — 5 check functions and their ALLOWED_SERVICE_NAMES list

_integration_notes.synthetic_case_use: When scoring the AI Doctor against synthetic patient records (Wave 6), a VerificationReport is produced for each trunk turn. pass=false means the scoring layer should not evaluate clinical correctness — the output was ungrounded. The scoring rubrics in management_plan_node and safety_netting_node (Files 13-14) are applied only to pass=true outputs.
